faqs.q: How long would my extension take, given your factory is in Blackburn?
faqs.a: Blackburn is around an hour from Liverpool, which is one of the reasons the process is so smooth here. Your extension is built in our factory in as little as four weeks while your home stays untouched, then delivered and installed on site in under a week. Compared with a traditional build, where trades come and go for months, most Liverpool homeowners find the whole experience far quicker and calmer than they expected.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Liverpool?
faqs.a: It depends on your property and what you want to build. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, but they don't apply everywhere — flats, maisonettes and homes in conservation areas, such as parts of the Sefton Park area or the Georgian Quarter, usually need a full application. We review your address and design at the start and handle the paperwork with Liverpool City Council on your behalf, so you always know where you stand before anything is built.

faqs.q: Can you install on a tight terraced street with on-street parking?
faqs.a: Yes — narrow streets and rear alleyways are part of everyday life in neighbourhoods like Wavertree and Anfield, and we plan every installation around them. During your site survey we assess access, crane positioning and delivery routes, and we arrange any temporary parking suspensions or permits needed for installation day. Because the modules arrive finished and installation takes less than a week, disruption to your neighbours is kept to a minimum.

faqs.q: What do Liverpool homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: Kitchens and open-plan kitchen-diners are the most popular choice, especially on terraces where a side-return or rear extension transforms how the ground floor works. Home offices are close behind, along with kids' playrooms and garden rooms that add flexible living space without touching the main house. Whatever the use, each extension is designed around your home and how your family actually lives.

heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Made for Liverpool
intro:
Liverpool's homes are full of character — the Victorian terraces of Wavertree and Anfield, the generous semis of Childwall, Woolton and Crosby, the elegant streets around Sefton Park and the Georgian Quarter. What many of them share is a familiar problem: not quite enough space for the way families live today. That's exactly what we solve.

EazyBase designs and delivers modular home extensions in Liverpool — new kitchens, dining rooms, home offices, playrooms and garden rooms that are precision-built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks, then installed at your home in under a week. No months of scaffolding, no skip on the street from spring to autumn, no builders in your kitchen.

Because your extension takes shape off site, your home and your routine stay intact until installation week. Then it arrives finished to a factory standard, and everyday life carries on — just with considerably more room in it.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Liverpool terrace
localAngles.body: The terraced streets of Wavertree, Anfield and much of inner Liverpool were built long before open-plan living, and the narrow galley kitchen at the back tells you as much. A side-return or rear extension is often the answer — reclaiming that awkward strip beside the kitchen or pushing out into the yard to create one bright, sociable space. Modular construction suits terraces particularly well: the extension is built to millimetre precision off site, so the on-site work squeezed between party walls is measured in days, not months.

localAngles.heading: More room for semis in Childwall, Woolton and Crosby
localAngles.body: Suburban semis across south Liverpool and up the coast to Crosby usually come with something terraces don't: a proper garden. That opens up options — a full-width rear extension for a kitchen-diner, or a standalone garden room that becomes a home office, playroom or quiet retreat. Because our extensions arrive largely complete, you keep the garden you love rather than surrendering it to a building site for half a year. Most of our Liverpool projects on semis are installed with the lawn still intact.

localAngles.heading: Planning and conservation areas in Liverpool
localAngles.body: Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions — but always check with Liverpool City Council, as the rules differ for flats, maisonettes and anything in a conservation area. Around Sefton Park, the Georgian Quarter and other protected streets, applications need extra care with materials, proportions and how the extension sits against the original architecture. We handle that thinking for you: our design team prepares the drawings, advises on what's realistic for your address, and manages the application so nothing is built before consent is clear.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works so well here
localAngles.body: Anyone who has lived through a Merseyside winter knows why building indoors makes sense. Traditional extensions stall when the weather turns; ours are built inside our Blackburn factory, about an hour from Liverpool, in a controlled environment where rain never stops play. Quality is checked at every stage rather than judged from a ladder, and your home stays sealed and secure until installation week. For terraced streets with tight access and neighbours close by, a build that arrives finished and goes in within days is a genuine relief.

metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Liverpool terraces and semis. Built in Blackburn in as little as 4 weeks, installed at your home in under a week.
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Liverpool | EazyBase
